parts.content:
Write 1 dissimilar SBA question(s) on: 'The Eleveld propofol model was derived from a large dataset across a broad range of ages and body sizes.'.

Each question should test application of knowledge, not simple recall — a candidate should need to reason from principles rather than just retrieve a fact. Use the retrieve tool to find relevant reference material before writing. Return the result as a QuestionSet.

parts.args: {"query": 'The Eleveld propofol model was derived from a large dataset across a broad range of ages and body sizes.'}

{"topic":"Final Frca Sample Sbas","exam":"final","curriculum_node_code":null,"model":"openai:gpt-5.5","generated_at":"2026-05-02T00:00:00Z","questions":[{"stem":"A 39-year-old male weighing 152 kg and measuring 180 cm is scheduled for laparoscopic gastric bypass under general anaesthesia. A propofol target-controlled infusion using the Eleveld model is planned.","lead":"Which feature of the Eleveld model best explains its improved dosing accuracy in this patient compared with the Marsh or Schnider models?","options":[{"text":"It adjusts the ke0 value upward in obese individuals to compensate for increased adipose tissue distribution","is_correct":false,"explanation":"The Eleveld model does not improve dosing in obesity by increasing ke0. In fact, ke0 tends to decrease with increasing age and obesity in the model, so this statement is inaccurate."},{"text":"It assumes a two-compartment model with slower distribution into peripheral tissues in obese individuals","is_correct":false,"explanation":"Eleveld is a three-compartment pharmacokinetic-pharmacodynamic model. Its strength is covariate-based and allometric scaling, not a simplified two-compartment assumption."},{"text":"It automatically limits total infusion rates when BMI exceeds 40, to avoid excessive plasma concentrations","is_correct":false,"explanation":"The Eleveld model does not apply an arbitrary infusion-rate cap based on BMI. It calculates doses from pharmacokinetic parameters adjusted continuously for patient covariates."},{"text":"It includes continuous covariate-based scaling of both volume and clearance using fat-free mass and applies allometric functions to account for obesity","is_correct":true,"explanation":"Eleveld incorporates continuous covariates including age, sex, height, weight and fat-free mass, and uses allometric scaling for volumes and clearances. This gives more robust predictions at extremes of body weight than models based on total body weight or fixed lean body mass equations."},{"text":"It uses fixed lean body mass estimates, thereby preventing overdose in obese patients","is_correct":false,"explanation":"Fixed lean body mass estimates are a limitation of the Schnider model, especially at extremes of body size. Eleveld avoids this by using continuous covariate and allometric scaling."}],"explanation":"The Eleveld propofol model was derived from a large dataset across a broad range of ages and body sizes. Unlike Marsh, which scales linearly with total body weight and may overdose obese patients, and Schnider, which uses lean body mass equations that may be unreliable at extremes, Eleveld uses covariate-based and allometric scaling of pharmacokinetic and pharmacodynamic parameters. This makes it better suited to morbidly obese patients undergoing TCI anaesthesia.","curriculum_node_codes":["2_GA_X_1"],"curriculum_node_scores":[0.5004564077727434]}]}
